jokes.text: If you liked the meal... just wait till you try these after-dinner mints.
jokes.setup: Romantic dinner setting, promising 'after-dinner mints' sounds pleasant
jokes.punchline: The mints are actually something disgusting based on Leela's reaction
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection setup with economical delivery, though the payoff relies on visual disgust

jokes.text: Ew!
jokes.setup: Doug's 'after-dinner mints' offer
jokes.punchline: Leela's disgusted reaction reveals the mints are something vile
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy but predictable payoff to the setup

jokes.text: I don't know. Something I couldn't quite put my finger on.
jokes.setup: Asked what was wrong with her date
jokes.punchline: 'Couldn't put my finger on' - literal reference to not wanting to touch him
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels with good character voice

jokes.text: Possibly his vile lizard tongue.
jokes.setup: Leela being vague about what was wrong with her date
jokes.punchline: Amy matter-of-factly identifies the specific gross detail
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail delivered matter-of-factly, good sci-fi dating humor

jokes.text: If you rule out every guy... with a lizard tongue or a low I.Q.... or an explosive, violent temper... of course you're going to be lonely.
jokes.setup: Amy telling Leela she's too picky
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly terrible traits as if they're minor dating preferences
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with absurd logic delivered earnestly - classic Futurama humor

jokes.text: The female Leela's problem... is purely medical. Soon she will drop her eggs and they will hatch... and all will be well.
jokes.setup: Discussing Leela's dating problems
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg misdiagnoses it as an egg-laying issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - only Zoidberg would say this, blends his medical incompetence with alien biology

jokes.text: Sometimes you meet a guy and think he's a pig... but then later on, you realize he actually has a really good body.
jokes.setup: Amy giving dating advice about giving guys a chance
jokes.punchline: Her 'deeper' realization is still completely shallow
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy characterization - appears to be making a profound point but reveals her shallowness

jokes.text: That sounds like a cry for help. Let's all take her out tonight.
jokes.setup: Leela says she's happy with her life
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately diagnoses this as pathological
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Amy's pushy nature and inability to accept Leela's contentment

jokes.text: The Federal Sex Bureau. A saucy puppet show. The rotting carcass of a whale.
jokes.setup: Listing good places to meet people
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and unsexy locations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from bureaucratic to absurd to grotesque, highly quotable Futurama worldbuilding

jokes.text: Why's everyone wearing those rings? Because nobody wears them anymore. Rings are stupid.
jokes.setup: Question about fashion trend
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of anti-fashion being fashionable
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about fashion trends and conformity through rebellion

jokes.text: Hey, did that lad just say rings are cool? Nope. He said they're stupid. Cool!
jokes.setup: Fry accidentally says rings are cool
jokes.punchline: Amy's quick lie saves him from being uncool
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that reinforces the absurd fashion logic

jokes.text: I've got what they call 'gaydar.'
jokes.setup: Amy claims to know if someone is gay
jokes.punchline: References the real-world concept of 'gaydar'
jokes.explanation: Dated reference that was probably funnier in 1999, relies on pop culture familiarity

jokes.text: Unless I'm getting interference from a gay weather balloon.
jokes.setup: Amy's gaydar detecting someone as gay
jokes.punchline: Treats gaydar like actual radar with technical interference
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation that treats the metaphor literally with sci-fi absurdity

jokes.text: Remember when those cyborgs enslaved humanity? Uh... yeah. That rings a bell.
jokes.setup: Bolt reminiscing about major historical event
jokes.punchline: Fry vaguely pretends to remember something he obviously wasn't conscious for
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's awkward attempts to fit in with future history

jokes.text: I think she means ten wins on the gay circuit. I wish. Those cats can really fly.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests Bolt is talking about gay racing
jokes.punchline: Bolt genuinely wishes he could compete in that superior circuit
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion where the 'insult' is taken as a compliment about superior competition

jokes.text: This is M-5438, an entity of pure energy.
jokes.setup: Introducing Leela to a potential date
jokes.punchline: The 'person' is literally just energy/light with no physical form
jokes.explanation: Creative sci-fi dating absurdity, though the joke relies heavily on visual

jokes.text: One day you will evolve beyond your physical body... and on that day... I hope you will pick up the phone.
jokes.setup: Energy being getting rejected by Leela
jokes.punchline: His pickup line is about evolutionary transcendence but ends mundanely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of cosmic philosophy with pedestrian dating language

jokes.text: I think he comes from a dimension... that's big on musical theater.
jokes.setup: Amy explaining why the energy being was rejected
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic way of calling him gay using dimensional origins
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism that fits the sci-fi setting while maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I thought you had two eyes.
jokes.setup: Guy approaching Leela romantically
jokes.punchline: Immediately backs off upon seeing her one eye
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that reinforces Leela's dating struggles with her unique appearance

jokes.text: you must've had your pick of the litter... last night at closing time.
jokes.setup: Bender assuming Leela had dating success
jokes.punchline: 'Pick of the litter' - treating desperate closing-time guys like animals
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender crudeness with good metaphor, fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: A delivery? No. A tax-deductible mission of charity.
jokes.setup: Crew expecting a normal delivery job
jokes.punchline: Professor frames animal rescue in bureaucratic/selfish terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor characterization - making even charity about personal benefit

jokes.text: each pound of which... weighs over 10,000 pounds.
jokes.setup: Explaining dark matter's properties
jokes.punchline: Circular definition that makes no scientific sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly nonsensical pseudo-science that sounds authoritative but is completely circular

jokes.text: Yes, but what about the animals? The what? The animals. I didn't say anything about animals.
jokes.setup: Professor supposedly caring about animals
jokes.punchline: He immediately forgets and denies mentioning them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment showing his complete disregard for the supposed mission

jokes.text: Why two? Oh.
jokes.setup: Noah's ark reference about bringing two of each animal
jokes.punchline: Fry slowly realizes it's for breeding, his delayed 'oh' of understanding
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry stupidity with perfect timing - the delayed realization is very in-character

jokes.text: What she ought to do... is find a nice guy with two eyes... and poke one out. Yeah. That'd be a time-saver.
jokes.setup: Discussing Leela's dating difficulties with her one eye
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting mutilation as a practical dating solution
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Bender logic with Fry's innocent agreement - shows both characters brilliantly

jokes.text: You can use this as an eye poker.
jokes.setup: Following up on the eye-poking suggestion
jokes.punchline: Bender helpfully provides a tool for the mutilation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Bender's helpful but twisted nature

jokes.text: As long as it's less than five.
jokes.setup: Leela saying she doesn't care how many eyes a man has
jokes.punchline: Sets an arbitrary upper limit that implies she's encountered 5+ eyed beings
jokes.explanation: Great worldbuilding joke that suggests the bizarre variety of aliens she's dated

jokes.text: I'm not as big a fan of velour as you are.
jokes.setup: Zapp commenting on their new uniforms
jokes.punchline: Kif's understated way of expressing his dislike for Zapp's obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif moment - polite disagreement that hints at his suffering under Zapp

jokes.text: Destroy them. Mmm... that's got a nice feel to it.
jokes.setup: Being asked about the unidentified ship
jokes.punchline: Zapp's casual, sensual enjoyment of ordering destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp introduction - showing his casual violence and bizarre sensuality in one line

jokes.text: Who are you talking to, sir? You.
jokes.setup: Zapp doing a captain's log style monologue
jokes.punchline: He was talking TO Kif, not making a log entry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Star Trek captain's log trope with Zapp's cluelessness

jokes.text: I'm anticipating an all-out tactical dogfight... followed by a light dinner. Ravioli, ham, sundae bar.
jokes.setup: Dramatic military planning
jokes.punchline: Transitions from warfare to mundane meal planning with same gravity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp characterization - treating dinner planning with same importance as battle strategy

jokes.text: In the game of chess... you can never let your adversary see your pieces. What?
jokes.setup: Zapp offering strategic wisdom
jokes.punchline: His chess 'strategy' makes no sense - pieces are always visible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Zapp moment - confidently stating nonsensical strategy that sounds profound but is completely wrong

jokes.text: A grim day for robotkind. But we can always build more killbots.
jokes.setup: Hearing about Zapp defeating killbots
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact attitude about robot genocide
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - showing robot solidarity but also pragmatic acceptance of replacement

jokes.text: Just in case you guys hit it off... you'll want to take this with you.
jokes.setup: Amy giving Leela something for her meeting with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Based on context, likely contraceptives or something embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Relies on visual gag and innuendo, fits Amy's character but predictable

jokes.text: Or my own masterwork... Zapp Brannigan's Big Book of War.
jokes.setup: Referencing Sun Tzu's Art of War
jokes.punchline: Zapp has written his own military strategy book with an ridiculous title
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Zapp's ego and terrible naming sense

jokes.text: If that wicker chair I like survives the slaughter... have it sent to my P.O. Box.
jokes.setup: Zapp dramatically preparing for defeat
jokes.punchline: His final request is about furniture delivery, not honor or legacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp - juxtaposing dramatic military defeat with petty material concerns

jokes.text: Has my fame preceded me, or was I too quick for it?
jokes.setup: Zapp introducing himself to Leela
jokes.punchline: Plays with the phrase 'fame preceded' as if it's a race he could outrun
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp pickup line with clever wordplay that reveals his narcissism

jokes.text: I doubt I've seen more than three or four captains... sexier than you... and only one who was deadlier.
jokes.setup: Zapp trying to compliment Leela
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that ranks her as maybe 5th sexiest captain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp - trying to be suave but revealing he's rating and comparing women

jokes.text: You see, killbots have a preset kill limit. Knowing their weakness... I sent wave after wave of my own men at them... until they reached their limit and shut down.
jokes.setup: Zapp explaining his famous military victory
jokes.punchline: His 'strategy' was just sacrificing his own soldiers until the enemy stopped working
jokes.explanation: Legendary Futurama moment - perfectly encapsulates Zapp's incompetent leadership and casual disregard for human life, became a cultural touchstone

jokes.text: You suck!
jokes.setup: Zapp asking his men to support his claim about sending waves of soldiers
jokes.punchline: Instead of support, his soldier directly insults him
jokes.explanation: Perfect punctuation to Zapp's horrific military strategy - shows what his men really think

jokes.text: Yes, by a Democratic Order of Planets mining crew.
jokes.setup: Leela pointing out the planet was already interfered with
jokes.punchline: Zapp admits the 'good guys' did the environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Good satire of bureaucratic double standards and environmental hypocrisy

jokes.text: I don't pretend to understand Brannigan's Law. I merely enforce it.
jokes.setup: Leela questioning the logic of Brannigan's Law
jokes.punchline: Zapp admits he doesn't understand his own law
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of blind authority and bureaucratic mindlessness - very quotable

jokes.text: Can I get this wrapped up?
jokes.setup: Dramatic scene of being imprisoned
jokes.punchline: Bender's only concern is taking his food to-go
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - completely missing the gravity of the situation, focused on practical concerns

jokes.text: You really are too picky.
jokes.setup: Leela complaining about Zapp throwing them in prison
jokes.punchline: Fry still thinks she's being too selective in dating
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Fry's obliviousness to the actual situation

jokes.text: You already imprisoned her under directive B10.81. You mean Brannigan's Law? Right. That law. Which one?
jokes.setup: Kif referencing the law by number
jokes.punchline: Zapp doesn't know which of his own laws Kif is talking about
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic showing Kif knows Zapp's work better than Zapp does

jokes.text: This time we are sure she's a woman, right?
jokes.setup: Zapp planning to seduce Leela
jokes.punchline: Implies Zapp has made this mistake before
jokes.explanation: Clever backstory implication that suggests Zapp's previous romantic failures

jokes.text: The boy, sir? You. You lay out my formal shorts.
jokes.setup: Zapp referring to someone as 'the boy'
jokes.punchline: He means Kif, his military colleague, treating him like a servant
jokes.explanation: Perfect power dynamic showing Zapp's casual humiliation of Kif

jokes.text: Welcome to my humble chamber... or as I call it... the 'love-nasium.'
jokes.setup: Zapp showing Leela his quarters
jokes.punchline: Combination of 'love' and 'gymnasium' creates a ridiculous term
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp portmanteau that perfectly captures his crude romantic style

jokes.text: Cham-pag-en?
jokes.setup: Zapp offering champagne
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'champagne' in an affected, pretentious way
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zapp's fake sophistication

jokes.text: I didn't realize you were such a coin-a-sewer. Well, I have studied abroad... or two.
jokes.setup: Leela sarcastically calling Zapp a 'connoisseur'
jokes.punchline: Zapp mishears and makes a crude pun about 'studying a broad'
jokes.explanation: Typical crude Zapp wordplay, fits character but predictable

jokes.text: Erotic. What? Erotic! Erotic! Erotic!
jokes.setup: Zapp finding Leela's serious conversation arousing
jokes.punchline: Repeats 'erotic' with increasing enthusiasm while she's horrified
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing Zapp's inappropriate responses to normal conversation

jokes.text: Come over here and feel my velour bedspread. I'm not really in the mood. Leela, it's real velour.
jokes.setup: Zapp's seduction attempt
jokes.punchline: He thinks mentioning the fabric quality will change her mind
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Zapp's complete misunderstanding of seduction

jokes.text: You'd rather sit in prison... than spend one evening with the Zapper?
jokes.setup: Leela wanting to go back to prison
jokes.punchline: Zapp's disbelief and referring to himself as 'the Zapper'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. 'The Zapper' nickname shows his self-aggrandizement.

jokes.text: What are you doing? Oh, God, I'm pathetic. Sorry. Just go.
jokes.setup: Zapp's failed seduction attempt
jokes.punchline: Sudden moment of self-awareness and vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character depth that makes Zapp briefly sympathetic

jokes.text: No. And it's pronounced 'champagne.' Oh, God, no!
jokes.setup: Zapp offering his mispronounced champagne
jokes.punchline: Leela corrects him, causing more embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to earlier joke, adds to Zapp's humiliation

jokes.text: I get so lonely. I just thought you, a fellow captain, would understand.
jokes.setup: Zapp's pathetic state after rejection
jokes.punchline: Appeals to professional solidarity to justify his behavior
jokes.explanation: Continues the unexpectedly vulnerable Zapp, making him manipulative but pitiable

jokes.text: Good morning, lover.
jokes.setup: Zapp greeting Leela the morning after
jokes.punchline: Reveals they slept together, shocking the audience and crew
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation delivered casually, creates shock and comedy

jokes.text: A sexy mistake. No, just a regular mistake.
jokes.setup: Leela calling their night together a mistake
jokes.punchline: Zapp tries to romanticize it, Leela firmly rejects this
jokes.explanation: Good back-and-forth showing their different perspectives on the encounter

jokes.text: The only kind of crawling I'm doing to you is away from.
jokes.setup: Zapp saying she'll come crawling back
jokes.punchline: Leela awkwardly restructures the phrase to make it about leaving
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback that shows Leela's attempt to be clever while flustered

jokes.text: Then you'll come crawling back... for another taste of sweet, sweet candy. Bam.
jokes.setup: Zapp predicting Leela will return
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself as 'sweet, sweet candy' with finger guns gesture
jokes.explanation: Peak Zapp cringe with the candy metaphor and physical gesture

jokes.text: I have made it with a woman. Inform the men.
jokes.setup: Zapp announcing his sexual encounter to Kif
jokes.punchline: Treats it like military intelligence that needs to be distributed
jokes.explanation: Legendary Zapp line - perfect blend of pompous announcement and pathetic achievement

jokes.text: The fatso says you're free to go. Really? Why? Why indeed?
jokes.setup: Guard announcing their release
jokes.punchline: Bender's ominous non-answer to Leela's question
jokes.explanation: Sets up mystery about their release. Bender's delivery adds intrigue.

jokes.text: We just talked, okay?
jokes.setup: Crew wondering why they were released
jokes.punchline: Leela's defensive denial while clearly something happened
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassment humor, shows Leela's shame about the encounter

jokes.text: The planet is supposed to collapse in approximately... two hours ago.
jokes.setup: Leela checking their timeline for the mission
jokes.punchline: They're already past the deadline, creating urgency and comedy
jokes.explanation: Good tension-building joke that shows how behind schedule they are

jokes.text: So you're saying we can cook him? Yeah, barbecue. I'll wear my hilarious apron.
jokes.setup: Finding an animal not on the protected list
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately wants to cook it and mentions his comedy apron
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - casual about cooking cute animals while excited about his apron

jokes.text: I don't care how hilarious your apron is. We're not cooking him.
jokes.setup: Bender's enthusiasm about his comedy apron
jokes.punchline: Leela's exasperated response acknowledges the apron while staying firm
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that plays along with the absurd detail

jokes.text: That's a shame, sir.
jokes.setup: Zapp saying he won't have time for the boys once his woman returns
jokes.punchline: Kif's polite but clearly sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif deadpan - polite on surface but dripping with sarcasm

jokes.text: once my woman returns... I won't have much time... to hang out with the boys anymore.
jokes.setup: Zapp talking to Kif about the future
jokes.punchline: His assumption that Leela will return and be 'his woman'
jokes.explanation: Shows Zapp's complete delusion about their relationship. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: He's pending for a bending.
jokes.setup: Nibbler eating all the rescued animals
jokes.punchline: Bender's threat using his name/function as a verb
jokes.explanation: Clever Bender wordplay that uses his robot identity for threats

jokes.text: It's not his fault... he's an unstoppable killing machine. Is it, snuggums?
jokes.setup: Defending Nibbler from Bender's anger
jokes.punchline: Calls him a killing machine while using baby talk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of terrifying description with affectionate baby talk

jokes.text: The planet's 'kerploding.'
jokes.setup: Planet about to explode catastrophically
jokes.punchline: Fry uses childish sound-effect word for explosion
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry - using juvenile vocabulary for serious situations

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll do it when we get back.
jokes.setup: Leela saying Bender should have filled the tank before leaving
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual procrastination despite their life-threatening situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response showing his complete lack of urgency or responsibility

jokes.text: Nothing. We just talked. So what's your problem? It's not like you slept with him. Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Fry innocently assuming nothing happened
jokes.punchline: His comment makes Leela realize her crew knows the truth
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment that confirms what the audience suspected

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Oh, how could you, Leela? I thought you had some standards. I mean, geez, he's a dumb gross gorilla.
jokes.setup: Learning Leela slept with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Amy criticizing Leela's standards despite her own promiscuous behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's hypocrisy. Her moral outrage is funny given her own behavior.

jokes.text: I thought you had some standards. I mean, geez, he's a dumb gross gorilla.
jokes.setup: Fry's shock at Leela sleeping with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Brutal but accurate description of Zapp
jokes.explanation: Harsh but accurate Fry assessment that voices what everyone is thinking

jokes.text: So crawling back to the Big 'Z' like a bird on its belly. Delicious.
jokes.setup: Leela calling for help
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor about birds crawling, plus savoring the moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp mixed metaphor showing his pompous but confused nature

jokes.text: Birds don't crawl. They've been known to.
jokes.setup: Leela correcting Zapp's bird metaphor
jokes.punchline: Zapp doubles down on his nonsensical claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - refusing to admit error by making an even more absurd claim

jokes.text: Can't you ask a little more sexfully?
jokes.setup: Leela asking for rescue
jokes.punchline: Zapp invents 'sexfully' as an adverb
jokes.explanation: Great Zapp neologism that perfectly captures his crude mentality

jokes.text: Brannigan's Law is like Brannigan's love... hard and fast.
jokes.setup: Zapp explaining his law
jokes.punchline: Compares law enforcement to his sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp - making everything about himself and his sexual prowess

jokes.text: You know, Zapp, once I thought you were a big, pompous buffoon. Then I realized that inside, you were just a pitiful child. But now I realize that outside that child... is a big, pompous buffoon.
jokes.setup: Leela's analysis of Zapp's character
jokes.punchline: Circular structure that ends where it started but with layers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular insult with perfect structure - appears to show growth but returns to original assessment

jokes.text: You know, Zapp, once I thought you were a big, pompous buffoon. Then I realized that inside, you were just a pitiful child. But now I realize that outside that child... is a big, pompous buffoon.
jokes.setup: Leela's evolving assessment of Zapp's character
jokes.punchline: Coming full circle to her original assessment with added layers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nested structure that builds complexity then returns to simplicity. Perfect character summation.

jokes.text: And which one rocked your world?
jokes.setup: Leela's elaborate insult about his character layers
jokes.punchline: Zapp misses the insult and asks which version pleased her sexually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp response - completely missing the elaborate insult and focusing on sex

jokes.text: So this guy just unloaded a steaming pile... of starship fuel?
jokes.setup: Discovering Nibbler's dark matter excrement
jokes.punchline: Describes defecation as 'unloading' valuable fuel
jokes.explanation: Good scatological humor that serves the plot while being appropriately crude

jokes.text: So a couple of animals... didn't die, and Leela got lucky. That's what I call a successful mission.
jokes.setup: Mission recap after saving some animals
jokes.punchline: Bender reduces it to minimal animal survival and Leela having sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender cynicism reducing a heroic mission to crude basics

jokes.text: I did find a cute little companion... who excretes starship fuel, and that's just as good.
jokes.setup: Leela reflecting on not finding love
jokes.punchline: Equates romantic companionship with a fuel-producing pet
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's practical approach to relationships

jokes.text: And in the end, is that not what man has dreamt of... since first he looked up at the stars? Kif, I'm asking you a question.
jokes.setup: Zapp making a profound philosophical statement
jokes.punchline: Has to specify it's a question because Kif isn't responding
jokes.explanation: Great ending showing Zapp's pompous philosophizing met with complete indifference from Kif
